text: Code refactoring is the process of improving a codebase's internal structure without altering its external behavior. Imagine cleaning a messy room—every item stays in place, but everything becomes more organized. This practice enhances readability, making the code easier for developers and teams to understand and maintain. Regular refactoring helps reduce technical debt, the hidden cost of taking shortcuts that slow down future development. By simplifying complex logic, removing duplication, or improving naming, developers create cleaner, more efficient code. Refactoring is not about adding new features; it is an investment in long-term software health that pays off with fewer bugs and faster development later.
